title: Neomammillaria wilcoxii
taxonomy: Neomammillaria wilcoxii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the cactus family, Cactaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Cactoideae and the tribe Cacteae. The genus Neomammillaria is a part of the Mammillaria Alliance, a group of closely related genera. Neomammillaria wilcoxii is further classified as part of the section Mammillaria section Mammillaria. Within the section, it is assigned to the series Mammillaria series Eumammillaria.
uses: Neomammillaria wilcoxii, also known as Wilcox's pincushion cactus, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. intro: Neomammillaria wilcoxii, commonly known as the Wilcox's pincushion cactus, is a species of flowering plant in the cactus family, characterized by its small, rounded stems and vibrant pink flowers. Traditionally, the plant is used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and wounds, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Neomammillaria wilcoxii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
